item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Man, the Foundation really sucks at containing conceptual phenomena. A teensy information leak is all it takes to ruin everything.

+1

_
| |_
| |_
| |_
| |_
| |_
|___________| Thanks everyone!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh yeah, that's definitely what I was aiming for. Just more "falling down stairs" than "people having sex" :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think it works well like that. The rest of the description has your general "peaceful" stuff like music and laughter, but then you read that which just feels slightly off and the whole scene suddenly reads differently.

Letting the Uncanny Valley carry the wham-line, per se.

item.title: Re: I warned you bro
item.content:encoded: Confirming for licensing that I am the individual who both took and allowed usage of the photograph used in the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
isn't necessarily referring to sex or similar?

…any sexual connotations are really, truly, genuinely unintended. Whoops.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know, I may need to take a step back.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I general have trouble wrapping my head on ontological, pataphysical, etc… writing, but I understood enough of this one to enjoy it and get an idea of what's going on. I think the short length helps with that. Couple minor questions, though.

Because of the inherent hazards posed by stairs, and the exacerbation of these hazards within SCP-4478

Is this saying that people smash down the stairs because they try to walk as if they weren't stairs? If so, that is hilarious and dark.

repetitive wet thuds.

Am I correct in inferring that this bit isn't necessarily referring to sex or similar?

Either way, +1. Had fun trying to work out the effects.

This staircase is now

n i c e

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I finally got it though, I feel ascended.
I WARNED YOU ABOUT STAIRS BRO

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so confusing, Ive been Stairing at it for a while now

item.title: Yes.
item.content:encoded:
I love articles like this. Just the right amount of mindf██k to be understandable, but still weird. Great references too, what with Zone Penrose. I would have asked about what the heck that last chart entry was supposed to mean, but then I realized that was the point. It’s vague in-universe as well.

but then i figured it out
collapse collapsible

the certification for zone penrose is 5/gradibus
google translate says gradibus is stairs in latin
zone penrose is related to stairs
that’s the breach

collapsed for mobile users
collapse collapsible

+
█
██
███
████
█████
██████

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a literary-ontological-rhetorical anomaly, which expands and grows wildly, dangerously, and unpredictably. The only way to contain it — that the Foundation's come up with, that is — is to trap it within something that possesses an entirely different semantic field. The way to artificially orchestrate this? Make sure that everybody who knows about this 'wrapper' concept — Zone Penrose — also understands that it bears no linguistic relation to the anomaly. Make this understanding involuntary and unambiguous, and Bob's your second degree male relative, you've now got a literary 'wall' through which the anomaly can't pass.

It's Keter because most of its effects are unknown, it's unpredictably dangerous (as stated previously), and may be able to affect things outside of its geographical location. Due to the nature of foreshadowing, however, this is not yet known.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Such a simple concept of an anomaly, yet written so well and wonderfully done
+1

Although I must say, I don't quite understand why the need for concealment of the anomaly in a spoiler, why its Keter or what the containment procedures imply we need a mnestic trigger to comprehend it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is so awesome. The actual anomaly itself is really interesting and the way you present the various properties is really well done, in a way that gives it more narrative weight than just a list of effects.

Stunning stuff. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well written and great idea. I love this so much. +1
Thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's finally up!
I loved this in chat and love it now

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’ll be honest, I don’t get it at all. I can appreciate that work was put into this but I can’t really upvote it due to my complete lack of understanding.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I now want to make this kind of anomaly a tag and will contribute as it gets some kind of name, mentioned both in this and the well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am so so stoked to finally see this posted.

+1. You never fail at coming up with surreal, mindfucky premises, and the execution of this one is no exception.

but you failed to heed my warning.
my warning about the regularly-elevated ambulation ascenders

item.title: I warned you bro
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to Advocate, Popsioak, Gee0765, NatVoltaic, Witryso, and probably some other folks.

Image was taken and provided to me by someone in #site19, with them giving explicit permission for me to use it. I am going to do my best to hunt them down because I can no longer remember their username. I'd appreciate it if licensing staff could give me a little temporal leeway here.

See veied's comment below :)

Spoilers: The stairs are a metaphor for stairs
